Focus on the enemy. It seems these traitors have been sharpening their blades for this ambush for a while now.
They have stirred the hearts of the Victorians. We are the only ones who can extinguish this rebellion now.
*Ancient Sarkaz Language*—
By the time he murmurs his covert orders, a group of silent figures are already appearing on the chaotic battlefield.
Like a passing gale rattling the raindrops in a storm.
Stand back.
Hmph. We'll handle these guys.
Julie, you and the rest keep going.
Got it.
Don't even think of getting close to the General.
No need to rush. It'll be your turn soon.
I am the General's guard captain, assassin.
Then you're gonna wanna hold your sword tight.
An inconspicuous arc sweeps past Julie's eyes.
A chill runs through her spine. Her body stiffens.
She thinks of the day that she saw Her Highness in the Scar Market.
At the time, she did not even dare to raise her sword.
Enough, Manfred.
General!
They have come to me in search of an honorable death, not mercy.
They are using their lives to show me the future as they see it.
You are in a race. She is facing countless threats in Kazdel, just as we are here.
Such a race only ends with my life, or hers.
I will give you a chance. Come, deathmongers. Kill me. Do not disappoint Theresa.

lines.st: Countdown
2 hours, 7 minutes, 21 seconds

lines.x: 'SHATTER'.
Th-The gates have fallen! They're about to come through!

lines.st: Countdown
1 hour, 33 minutes, 48 seconds

lines.x: Notify all operations within the city to disarm the patrolling troops.
The defenses in the city are weak. It's pretty much just the Military Commission's defense forces.
We'll strike at them before they can regroup. Move fast.
Even if both sides are trying to prevent the battle from spreading into the city, this is still rather irregular.
Send all our intelligence back to the Doctor. All operating teams, quicken your pace, and stay vigilant at all times.
Take down the Military Commission's flag in the plaza, and tune our radio into all frequencies in the city.

lines.st: Countdown
49 minutes, 59 seconds

lines.x: Prepare for the landship's connection. We want to let Her Highness and the others enter the region that we control ASAP.
Also contact Ascalon's team and tell them to rendezvous quickly. Three teams have already completed their deployments ahead of schedule.
Where to next, Dr. Kal'tsit?
The former site of Babel.
Babel's flag has not been raised there for many years.
Kal'tsit walks along the street. She's still familiar with the route to Babel's offices.
The Sarkaz on the street quietly move about, climbing as high as they can on the surrounding buildings, gazing at the same ruins within the city.
A flag that has not been seen in Kazdel for many years slowly rises.
Babel has returned.
Mantra's latest report says we've managed to completely clear five main roads.
If everything is going according to plan, Kal'tsit should have seen the fires of the Soul Furnace by now.
Kazdel's defenses are all but crippled, Doctor. We can board the city now.
Good.
But the Military Commission will not give up on Kazdel. If we want complete control of the city, we still need a bit more time.
By then, we might not be able to completely avert the destruction of Kazdel's center city—
Wait.
I felt them.
What?
They are still here, but they have no intention of aiding anyone.
It seems that some arrogant bystanders have joined our battle.
Theresa.

Hmm...
I also feel... enemies. Not ordinary ones.
Their souls burn with killing intent, sorrow, misery and determination.
The Military Commission's assassins. They intended to use the city as bait all along, so they can inflict heavy damage on Babel's headquarters.
This was also within my calculations. But this time, there is no wagering or planning our way out of this. We must defeat the assassins, then return to our city.
I will go control the landship's defense systems and mount a defense.
Go, Doctor.
We need not bring another fight into the city, not as it bathes in the twilight afterglow.

lines.st: Countdown
22 minutes, 48 seconds

lines.x: Closure, have you made contact with the landship?
Still no response.
I-I'm still overriding the city's broadcast power settings, and trying to expand its range. Maybe the landship's comms have a temporary malfunction... I'm doing my best to bring it back online!

lines.st: Countdown
12 minutes, 57 seconds

lines.x: I sense something peculiar, Dr. Kal'tsit.
My mother... She's prepared incantations for every person dear to her heart. When they pass away, an elegy will play in our minds.
I am still in good health at present, but...
Kal'tsit suddenly feels her blood run cold.
Even if Babel can capture Kazdel, it is bound to suffer grave losses.
Theresis was right. They can't unite. The division of the Sarkaz will be the new norm. Even given a long time, the Sarkaz are unable to go from a weak community to a world leader.
Most importantly, I pointed out another path to Theresis. His plans in Londinium will have a greater chance of success if the Sarkaz corrupt the land with Originium and destroy their enemies.
Isn't this a good thing?
Such a determined race is now our followers. They will become the first brilliant bloodstain on the curtain of Originium as it spreads.
That's right... We have no need to destroy them. No need to kill her.
I... should use the 'Civilight Eterna'. I should try to guide its hidden functions, and then I wouldn't need to...

lines.st: Searching internal array. Administrator permissions found.
Verified. Valid permissions.
Welcome, Dr. Doctor.
Entering (Shipwide Defense System). Please confirm subsequent operation.
Should I really let Babel continue their actions at this point?

Am I really going to renounce the sacrifices of billions of our people in the distant past, renounce their flesh and blood, ballads and songs, renounce the spark that they have waited for ten thousand years, renounce the Originium Project... all for a race who doesn't even know what the barrier is?
If, for the sake of Terra, Originium was stopped... If Theresa truly succeeds...
Then what have we been waiting for during the ten thousand years?
Theresa and the races of Terra... Do they really not have even the slightest chance of success?
They do not. How could I be so naive as to believe that? They are disunited, narrow-minded, undeveloped... An accident of life, a fortunate survival, that's all.
All lives have the right to believe in their own hopes.
But we have no time left.
It is right in front of us.

lines.x: ...Doctor?
Doctor! Can you hear me?
That's strange. There are some... peculiarities in the Doctor's emotions.
Intense, and... shadowed.
Doctor, what exactly...?

lines.st: Look.
There is the fiery light from the torn apart portal, like a smoldering gash across the sky.
Only after centuries will it dissipate into nothingness.
The stars are leaving from where we stand, like leaves trembling in the night.
Yet that ship from Talos-II still arrived at these rubble-strewn ruins.
That is life.
That is what we lost after our final day. That is the ancient ode to dispel any darkness.
To this day, we still do not understand how life appeared in this vast sea of stars.
But I think we should sing the praises of all that lives, and sing our own praises as well.
Originium is our song, the melody we offer to those who come after. Meddling with the evolution of civilization is arrogance, but I had no other choice.

lines.x: I have reneged on my promise. I have betrayed she who waited within time itself.
It is because of the love I felt. Love for life. Love for existence. Love is eternally pure. It leaves me unthinking.
Kal'tsit. Learn to love. Learn to believe. You have to think. Go and lead. Go and face the rolling stones that fall down the hills, screaming as they go.
But in the end... I believe that you can learn to love. It is eternally pure. It is the child of every breathing creature. It is our nature.
Go. Kal'tsit. Go see your surroundings, then see what lies by the most distant of mountains. Go seek what forms existence takes. I must go back.
She had once taught me everything, once explored everything with fervor. But she has changed. She will not give me much time to act of my own accord.
May we meet again, next time. We will meet again. Kal'tsit.

opts: I believe in them. I believe in you, Kal'tsit.
Kal'tsit. I do not have much time left.
Search for traces of life. Search for hope and a future.
Kal'tsit... Go find your own answer.

lines.st: Are you sure you want to disable the Shipwide Defense System?

lines.x: Doctor—!

lines.st: (Restart boarding ID verification) selected. Confirmed.
(Restart Shipwide Communications Modules) selected. Confirmed.
Loading...
Countdown has reached zero.

lines.x: The fluorescence of the control panel reflects off the Doctor's mask.
As the screen shuts down, the face beneath the mask is shrouded in darkness again.

lines.st: How would you describe those who would pay any price to defend their people?
Some would call them heroes. Some would call them legends.
But such titles best take root in times of widespread war.
As a short era of peace begins, in the space of a night, those living legends will all be relegated to the land of stories and tales.
The children who hear such tales will ask: why have all the people who bear such grand titles disappeared?

lines.x: Because they chose to stay in the past.
They had seen through their duty, and the new era they founded has no place for them.
Legends are forgotten. Heroes pass out of memory.
And death arrives as it always does. None can escape it.
Duty spurs a hero to write an answer with death, to tread a muddy road.
That is our fate.

lines.st: The eve of Babel's departure from Kazdel

lines.x: Perhaps after many years, those who were left behind and vanished after we built our home will join us in our causes.
They will bear a name wracked with infamy and settle this debate for us.
Perhaps.
The twins stand shoulder to shoulder.
By means of the memories stored within the Black Crown, they walk along the battlefield of yesteryear. Just as it was in the past.
The two mixed-blood Sarkaz step off the King's collapsed chariot, walking toward the battle...
Walking toward the future.
So is this where we part ways?
It is. Here, where we joined hands to change Kazdel, we part.
Even though you have been their King for so many years, at times... you are as you were back then.
Back when we were still young, I would often prick my fingers as I was sewing, staining the robes I would sell. Do you remember?
Yes...
At the time, you were still a gloomy recruit, not allowed to wield a real sword, always dreaming of becoming a soldier of Kazdel one day.
I, for my part, still did not understand why we had to hide wherever we went.
But then came the day that the Royal Court took note of my work, and I got to meet Yliš. During that meeting, I summoned my courage to ask the King of Sarkaz—
Where does our future lie?
He had no answer, only bidding me to leave.
Only once I saw our defeated troops return to their homeland in ruins did I finally understand. The answer I sought could not be found in other lands, but 'here'—
—In 'Kazdel'.
Kazdel will only return to glory when one of us is gone.
But the one who is destined to lose is you. Must you persist with this, Theresa?
There are many by my side who support me.
Most Sarkaz who have suffered oppression and humiliation choose rebellion and violence.
No matter if those who remain wish it or not, they will eventually be swept along by the tides and drawn into wars.
But our generation must do something. I do not wish for this hatred in my hand to pass on to the next, where it will continue to fester and grow anew.
Even if I cannot change the future, the hope that will ignite the spark must be carried forward.
I too must take charge of those who follow me.
The cries answering the war do not come solely from Kazdel. There is no place for us anywhere in these lands.
All I can do is give them a push.
And so I will stay here. I choose to stay in the past, where the new generation of Sarkaz will one day look back.
Just like you.
Outside Rhodes Island of Babel, Kazdel
Babel is defenseless, as the Regent said.
Keep quiet. Stay alert.
Stick to the original route and find the King. A Route and B Route, stand by.
Their main force is not on the ship. They will need time to call for support.
Babel's guards still haven't noticed us.
His Highness was right. There is someone high up in the enemy's chain of command helping us.
All units, enter the ship. We settle this quickly. Avoid anything unexpected.
Ten minutes ago, Amiya was roused by a strange sound.
Babel is continually being maintained and renovated. It has already seen massive changes compared to when she first arrived at her new home.
'Maybe Closure is busy fixing things again?'
The Doctor left Amiya many picture books in her room, with illustrated stories she had never heard of before.
Her favorite is one that tells of someone who falls from the sky riding a fantastical vehicle, and the many adventures that come of it.
But she is not fond of the ending. The hero repairs the vehicle and has to return to the sky, saying goodbye to the children on the ground.
I really don't like all this noise. The Doctor still isn't here...
Theresa... I need to go find Theresa.
Hello, Mr. Downing! You dropped your pliers on the ground. Here!
Thank you, Amiya. Where are you going?
I'm going to the Speaker's Office to find Theresa. Oh, right. Have you seen the Doctor?
The Doctor? It's been a few days. That one ought to be in the Command Center overseeing the operation, I think?
I knew the Doctor must be busy with something... Okay. Bye-bye!
Don't run so fast, Amiya! There's still a lot of places being overhauled on the ship, so make sure you don't trip!
That girl really is—
Urgh...
This one's handled. Proceed.
Our men on the inside are already on standby at every ambush spot. We can begin a coordinated assault at any time.
If you see anyone, don't hesitate. Those left on the ship are no threat.
I brought my book, Theresa—
Huh? Why is the door shut?

Theresa, it's me! Are you in there?
Closure once taught me another way to open the door...
It's open!
Theresa—
What are you doing here, Amiya?
I heard some loud noises that sounded really close... I got a little scared.
We can go home soon.
You look tired...
Would you like to stay with me while I work?
Is this... a storybook?
I see. Well, as long as it's for you, I think I can take a short break. The Doctor and Dr. Kal'tsit won't mind.
Thank you, Theresa!
Alright, come sit over here. Once we finish this story, I'll take you to my homeland to play.
So, where did we leave off last time?
Stand back, Duq'arael.
You are covered in the blood of the mediocre.
Merely some heirs making ridiculous comments on the situation. Nobody should be making light of that pair of siblings' lives or deaths at this moment.
It is a grave disrespect.
Should you not be retreating and defending the inner city right now, Nezzsalem?
That has nothing to do with the war I am waiting for.
Theresis asked you to come and ensure her death?
Is this not you being contemptuous of those siblings yourself? The Regent would not stoop to such methods.
I am only here to witness the end of this war, and the beginning of another.
If the King of Sarkaz is struck down, I will bear the deathly pall of this moment. On behalf of the King, I will witness the victory I grasp from the abyss.
So, you still feel sorrow.
As do you.
The wreath of reeds slowly sinks into the riverbed. The gloomy mists have not dispersed for days.
Aefanyl... Are you still by her side?
For whom does the elegy ring out?
Why did you stop, Theresa? We just got to the important part of the story!
I thought I'd leave a little suspense for you, Amiya. Would you mind if I used another, more comfortable way to tell you this story?
But... Okay.
Watch out, Amiya.
A bubble! Huh? This is—
Black ripples continuously wash over the pure white room, as though Amiya has plunged into a pool filled with ink.
The black bubbles cut off all sound, just as they obscure Amiya's sight.
Beyond the bubbles, Theresa's figure becomes more and more indistinct.
Retreating further into the distance.
There...sa...?
Stay inside for now, Amiya. Sleep well.
You will be safe by my side.
*sigh*...
Theresa. Your Highness...
We will use our lives as proof of the future that the General pursues.
No, I was sighing about something else.
Honestly, there is no need to cover your face in front of me.
I still remember you from back when you accepted Yliš's orders before his chariot. Your heroism and valor in that battle moved me.
I remember most of your fellows as well.
You are all worthy of the title of 'brave warrior', in no way inferior to those heroes fighting in Kazdel this very moment.
We aren't covering our faces to hide our identities. We knew they would do nothing here.
We have cut off our horns, abandoned our names, even destroyed every inch of our skin that can be used to identify us. All so that after we use such wretched methods to kill the King of Sarkaz—
We will not be remembered by the Sarkaz. We can't be remembered.
Do it.
The rushing air engulfs the assassins' blades like a swamp, as they incessantly stab forwards.
Swoosh—
The trembling of the blades causes the air surrounding them to resonate. They press on. They continue to stab.
The ones holding the blades are buffeted and submerged by waves they cannot resist.
I remember your names.
I remember you. Luca.
You held back a Steam Knight on your own. You hid yourself within the steam to pull them out, and beheaded them before friend and foe.
The assassin's life fades away.
I remember you too. Liens.

Out of the countless Sarkaz with that name, you are the one who stood out most. After being taken captive by Spire Casters, you freed three of your compatriots from their camp and destroyed their Arts Circle.
Another death, and yet it cannot stop them from approaching Theresa.
And then you. Levin.
You took an arrow for Theresis, losing an arm in the process, but survived hell itself until you managed to retreat.
I do not wish for you all to die nameless and unknown.
At the very least... I will remember.
More and more corpses pile up by her side, while the room she has left to stand is whittled away, little by little.
She sighs, wiping the blood from her neck, accidentally smearing the dark red across her white dress.
We want to do something for the Sarkaz before we die. And so now we stand before you...
We have come to kill you. The King we once swore an oath of loyalty to.
We know your strength better than anyone. So, allow us damned traitors to escort you to hell.
The last thing Amiya remembers is sleeping inside the black bubble.
Theresa gently cradling the bubble wrapped around her.
Telling her a story that she has already heard many times before.
Within the Desert of Despair live many terrible shadows, forever chasing after the little puppet who is seeking for hope.
The black-cloaked shadows were the echoes of the oldest despair and sadness.
If they catch you, you'll never escape them.

Within the desert, the little puppet met the king, who was covered in pure gold and precious gems. The king told the little puppet that if he went back the way he came, he would be given riches beyond measure.
Despite the king pulling him back, the little puppet ignored the captivating gems, and pressed onwards.
But very soon—
The black-cloaked shadows caught him. They kept throwing themselves at him, over and over again, near endlessly...
'Bang, bang, bang'...
The puppet took out a long spear made of a needle and some threads, fighting the shadows, until finally, after knocking aside the shadows' sharp talons, he managed to escape.
He ran as fast as he could until he reached a large river of tears. There was neither boat nor weeds, so all the little puppet could do was swim across.
But as he soaked in the tears, he began to sink, until he touched the riverbed.
He had failed.
Was this how the story ended? Amiya could not believe.
Maybe Theresa read it wrongly?
She anxiously wished for Theresa to change the ending of the story.
Theresa...
...Yes?
Can we... save the little puppet?
You wish to save him?
I do. I... I really, really do.
All sound fades away.
The bubble obstructing Amiya's sight dissipates.
Theresa's figure becomes clearer and clearer—
Out of the corner of her eye, she sees dark red seep out of the black-cloaked shadows lying down on the ground.
Who are these people? When did they arrive here? Why are they lying on the ground?
Amiya had so many questions she wanted to ask Theresa. She did her best to open her eyes wide.
She sees a white dress stained crimson. She sees Theresa's bloodshot eyes.
Was Theresa okay?
Amiya wants to ask Theresa about her injuries.
But she could not make a sound.
It's okay, Amiya.
I am here. I beat those bad people from the stories.
What happened to Theresa?
Amiya wants to wipe the weariness off of Theresa's face.
But she could not move her hands.
I'm sorry. I can't change the ending of the story.
But you can, Amiya.
You will walk along a difficult path. Just like the little puppet who tried to cross the river of tears.
All I can do to help you is bestow this crown of black upon you.
For ten thousand years, the limitations imposed by history led my people to misuse this crown.
It has been stained. It has been bound. But it should not have been so.
If we are to break this curse, to break fate itself... I need your help.
Do you want to rewrite the ending?
Amiya wants to say 'I do', but she cannot utter a word.
She anxiously nods her head, thinking that perhaps she could help Theresa by saying yes.
Perhaps she could save her—
Though there is not much strength left in me, it is enough for me to transfer its permission to you.
I have sealed most of it away, so that its boundless information does not overwhelm your mind.
Forgive me, Amiya... for making you bear the weight of this crown.

I thought... that I could be there for you much, much longer. That I could watch you grow up. That I could stand with you when you bravely faced your parents' passing. That I could hear you answer my question yourself...
Your answer as to whether or not you wished to follow in my footsteps.
But there's not enough time left for us, Amiya.
I now selfishly consign this weight that I bear on to you.
I am sorry, child.
Amiya tries to scream, but she cannot open her mouth, cannot move her limbs.
A black blade seems to penetrate her chest.
She ought to feel pain, but this blade leaves behind—
Only warmth. A warmth brimming with hope.
Amiya. It is hope, and also pain.
You will be confused. You will hesitate.
But please, trust Dr. Kal'tsit. Trust that she will always stand by your side.
She will help you dredge back up the secrets of survival.
You can bear it, can't you, Amiya...?
You have always been a strong girl. One that never ceases to amaze me.
*sigh*...
I am so very tired.
Thank you, Amiya, for the joy you have given me, ever since the day we met.
Now rest for a while. Sleep.
Sleep in peaceful slumber.
I will always be with you.
Theresa hears a familiar voice.
Amiya!
Theresa does not raise her head to look at the one who just arrived.
The crown has left her and found another. Her breath grows feeble, and feebler still.
Doctor... There was no need for you to come.
I felt... your emotions. I saw... your past.
You could have waited elsewhere, waited for my death to come. You knew that this time I would not escape, nor retreat.
Are you here for Amiya? Or perhaps...
Theresa does not need to hear the answer.
Behind the one she asked, a tide of assassins pour out of the shadows, rushing toward Theresa—
So that is your answer, Doctor...
Theresa is glad to see Amiya asleep.
Amiya did not see the Doctor's betrayal, nor Theresa's final bout of sorrow.
The Black Crown finally lands neatly in Amiya's hands as Theresa takes down the final assassin in the room.
It's alright, Amiya. Everything's fine...
It's over.
Theresa does not look at the one frozen in place before her. She gently pats the back of the child in her arms and leaves a kiss on her puerile cheek.
Theresa. I can't throw away this opportunity I spent millions of lives and myriads of years to reach.
I can't let you ruin the plan for Originium. I can't... let you turn this great land into a beautiful but fleeting dream.
And that's why you put Amiya in harm's way.
...Seeing as the King of Sarkaz has already seen my memories, I believe there's no need for any further explanation.
Do you hate me?
You hurt Amiya. You're about to hurt Kal'tsit, and the fighters who trust you so deeply.
Is this how you treat those lives?
I'll do what I can to ensure their safety in the ensuing conflict. This is what Theresis swore to me as well.
We only... need you to die.
Doctor... did you present yourself before me out of guilt?
Guilt... is too light of a word, compared to everything I've done.
We made too many choices in a time that even Kal'tsit isn't familiar with.
I just...
When Amiya grows up, if she remembers me, if she asks about me, will you tell her the truth... of how I left her?
No.
I'll help them search for another way to live.
You'll help them search for a final moment of peace before Originium destroys Terra?
I'm sorry... That's the one thing that's set in stone.
You know it's futile... and yet you still wish to cure Amiya's sickness.
Even if her family and homeland will all turn into the same hunks of Originium in the not-too-distant future, along with anyone and anything you'll ever know...
You still don't want the girl before your eyes to suffer.
That's what you sincerely feel. Your purest thoughts. Your innate nature.
And actually... I'm glad, Doctor.
I'm glad you're real and true—the one who Kal'tsit willingly trusts, and in turn trusts us.
You... the creator of Originium... Doctor. You are the poison that ruins everything, but also the antidote to it all.
...What?
Theresa strains to stand up, stretching her arms towards the Doctor, instilling in the latter an instinctive urge to retreat.
The Doctor tries to move, but fails. Sorrow far surpasses the collapsing rationality at this moment.
Theresa touches the face beneath the mask.
You're crying, Doctor...
Well, that's the kind of person you are. You know your own fragility, you know how much of a threat I pose to you even at a time like this... and yet... you still came here.
So, I still... am willing to believe in you.
To trust in who you really are.
To trust in our Doctor who brings the rain, dew, and sun.
This face is both familiar and strange. Theresa knows its features well.
She never had a chance to directly perceive the emotions on it until now.
She smiles and stretches out her finger, snipping the scene before her into two, as if it were made of cloth.
Then, she slowly pulls out a loose thread from the rift, and the remnants unravel as the Doctor's memories dissipate.
Theresa goes in deeper, without looking back, until every memory is taken apart into a mess of disconnected white threads.
Like the very night, a long time ago, when she was still young, teaching herself to sew with Theresis by her side.
The memories disintegrate until a mere fragment of the soul remains.
A silhouette stands at the end of that memory. Theresa thinks the figure both familiar and strange.
It's the Doctor. It's not the Doctor.

lines.st: What are you doing to me, Theresa?

lines.x: We're done here, but I suddenly feel like leaving you a surprise... so don't blame me for this, Doctor.
I'll say to you this one thing...
'Find yourself.'
Theresa holds the hands of the one before her, as if it were their first meeting.
She quietly faces the other pair of eyes, looking at the emotions shifting within until finally, only a void remains.
She knows then, every memory has been wiped clean, the soul purified.
Your memory is gone, and your soul reborn...
A truly terrible trick...
But they underestimate your benevolence, your desire for victory. They underestimate us, and what makes Terra so tenacious.
Doctor. Allow me to call you by this name for the last time...
This is my little revenge on you.
And also... my final gift to you.
Memory does not shape who you are. So, with your original self...
Live again... on this land. See the world, feel the various emotions life brings, meet the many people of Terra...
Perhaps... your journey will start in suffering, and you may never manage to escape the murky moon shadows of this land, but you will always... get to see the sun rise.
I trust you will find a new solution. One that is truly your own.
An answer that... sounds just like the murmur Kal'tsit and I once heard...
A most moving answer.
Life slips away.
Her ears gradually pick up an elegy from afar. Laqeramaline did establish a connection with her after all.
Theresa feels her own breath growing feebler, but she also feels unprecedentedly relieved.
She tries her best to keep Amiya in her arms, protected. Amiya's heartbeat calms her own.
I'm... a little tired. It's exhausting, holding onto that crown.
I should rest when I'm tired. Isn't that right, Amiya?
I once imagined that...
Perhaps, one day... We'll be able to let everyone on this vast land fall into peaceful slumber.
It may take a long time to come, but maybe, you could see it for me...
Amiya, when you wake up...
You'll have to keep going.
*sigh*—I'm so tired...
No, she's not here yet...
She clings to her final strand of remaining strength.
She is waiting for someone she must see.
Fortunately, the sound of her footsteps already draws near.
And then finally, even though she no longer has the strength to keep her eyes open, she knows—
She is here—
Kal'tsit...
Kal'tsit.
Theresa...
Amiya... Doctor...
I knew full well this possibility always existed... Why...?
I should have stayed behind—it's my...
The...resa.
I... We should have...
Theresa... confirmed... dead.
Mon3tr, heed my command...
Prepare... for any follow up attacks.
The Doctor and Amiya... nothing must happen to them. Protect them at all costs!
Mon3tr—!
Kal'tsit...
Kal'tsit.
Amiya opens her eyes, pink rhombuses faintly visible in her pupils.
Theresa's eyes remain shut tight.
But Kal'tsit knows.
It's her.
Kal'tsit... I've been waiting for you.
Theresa! I—
It's alright, Kal'tsit. It's fine.
Listen to me—
I'm already dead.
What you see now is my consciousness put into the crown. I'm borrowing the body of this girl to bid you farewell.
Kal'tsit, tell me. Did you raise Babel's banner high up in the sky over Kazdel?
I sent it back to Kazdel, and watched it ascend myself...
Thank you.
I came back too late, I knew there might be a possibility...
Don't blame yourself for this, Kal'tsit. You had your own mission to fulfil.
And... I managed to hold this long for you, and that's enough.
...Are we, running out of time to say our goodbyes?
I'm sorry, Kal'tsit...
...I understand.
Please don't blame yourself for this.
We trusted the Doctor, and I hope you can continue on our path into the future.
Kal'tsit, there's no need for any further misgivings.
I took a life, but I also saved it.
So please, take it to where it should be, if it can wake again...
Guide it, and it will in turn lead us to the end of our path—
I have no doubt about that.
I understand.
However... we can no longer take the risk of venturing into... the depths of Rhodes Island.
I will entrust someone I know to send the Doctor to Ursus.
Chernobog will be safe. It is too... dangerous to allow the Doctor to stay on Rhodes Island.
That's good... Kal'tsit. I'll leave the rest to Amiya then.
She will walk a difficult road.
I will be with her, and Babel... as well.
She is so young, but so strong.
Kal'tsit, giving the crown to Babel... to her, I did not do this on a selfish whim.
To break the cycle and deal with what is to come... The King cannot only be a King of the Sarkaz, nor can it only be a King of Terra.
The role is no fetter, controller, or unparalleled power.
The role is a key. The King is... 'Civilight Eterna'.
But Kal'tsit, keep this a secret from Amiya. She doesn't need to bear all of that now.
She will understand once she embarks on that path, she needs to understand—
Only by overcoming every obstacle can we find the doors that lead to this world.
...And you?
I will be gone in the pursuit of my ideal. As for her...
She will become the flame that burns all corruption to ashes.

lines.st: 'Kal'tsit, a new war is about to begin.'
'Theresis, my kin...'
'He will descend unto the cradle in Victoria that belongs to us, and Originium will cover that patch of land...'

lines.x: She is leaving us.

lines.st: 'He will bring suffering to the other races on this land...'

lines.x: While you will become the final struggle for her ideals.
A last-ditch effort.

lines.st: 'He will make everyone else on Terra see the Sarkaz as enemies once more.'

lines.x: Deathmongers—
Do not attempt to abandon your efforts, before her end is set...
Prove to me that her path still holds the chance to win a future—

lines.st: 'Prevent that day from turning true, before everything becomes irreversible...'

lines.x: Prove to her the peace you strive for is still worth a response.
Though I regret to say this, my compatriots...
The call to repay this blood debt is already resonating throughout this great land—

lines.st: 'Kazdel's hatred should not continue to spread.'

lines.x: Her death has been determined.
You have lost a Sarkaz King.
I have lost my only kin.
And I will not let these sacrifices be in vain.
My people—
Whether you've come for peace or vengeance—
I will hear your voices.

lines.st: 'Revenge. War. Survival. Future.'

lines.x: You will see where the Sarkaz's true path lies.
The blood with which you stain the steps of Victoria will eventually return to where it belongs—
We must make this place a new beginning for us.

lines.st: 'I can still hear the Sarkaz voices even now, Kal'tsit...'
'The closer I get to the myriad souls, the clearer their voices become.'
'What I can glean from their shouts and cries is—'

lines.x: Suffering will descend once more—
All of Terra will surrender—
Starting from this very spot.
The Sarkaz—

lines.st: 'The Sarkaz—'

lines.x: —Will rise again.

lines.st: '—Will ruin the land.'

lines.x: We have to stop him, Kal'tsit.
But... that's not something Babel can do, as it is now.
You'll find a way.
Besides, you'll never be alone. With the Babel we built together—

lines.st: 'Our companions will help you.'

lines.x: Your Highness—
Theresa...! Your Highness...! Your Highness!

lines.st: 'They wield a fierce determination.'

lines.x: ...Your Highness...
I'm back, Your Highness...
You would always welcome me back here when I returned.
But this time... you're not here.
Can you still hear me...?
Why couldn't you wait a little longer...?
...Damn it! Damn it all!
I'll make them pay... this I swear to you.
Every single one...
Even if it's... even if—
Master... Theresis—
Theresis...
He must face me... I... I have to find him!
All traitors... will suffer...
Your Highness Theresa... Theresis...
My... only two... I couldn't...
Clouds cover the setting sun, and Ascalon is shrouded in shadow.
She has lost her light, her faith, forever.
She struggled. She strived.
Only death remains for the one born in the storm.

lines.st: 'They are the most remarkable of all.'

lines.x: ...Logos.
...Yes.
We... I don't know... we should...
Let's retreat first.
Let's... return to Babel.

lines.st: 'They will never doubt our ideals, even for a second.'

lines.x: ...It's over.
Why...? Why I feel...
I too am baffled, King of the Nachzehrers.
I thought I was long accustomed to a King of Sarkaz's demise.

lines.st: 'Those—the ones left behind, you do not need to question their loyalty.'

lines.x: ...No way.
Who sent this *Sarkaz expletive* comm? Did the Military Commission intercept this? Are they screwing with us?!
I don't believe it... I... Shut the hell up! Scout!
The landship... should be this way!
Theresa... she couldn't have just... died like that!!
Winter of 898, The King of Sarkaz dies.
Yliš dies and Kazdel falls into ruin.
The explosion of the Soul Furnace sends dust flying all across Kazdel's territory for three whole days and nights...
Finally, it falls to the ground like black snowflakes.
Burying the bones of the invaders.
Along with the ruin once known as 'Kazdel'.
Kazdel... is no more.
But Kazdel has never been gone for good.
That's what I heard from the King of Sarkaz. He said Kazdel has met with ruin more than three thousand times, but it will always eventually be rebuilt anew.
Perhaps that was just a ploy by Yliš to deceive the people.
He's dead, Theresis.
But even if it were only a tale he spun while wasting away in the palace... I could, at the very least, sense a strand of longing from his stories.
A yearning for a stable life.
What happens after victory? Now, everyone will only begin wandering the land once more...
Our people will praise us as heroes, but we can only return to ruins devoid of life.
What can we even do now?
'Heroes'... Once this war is over, that title will be meaningless...
Indeed.
Also... I seem to have picked up the myriad souls whispering in my ear from a while ago...

Their voices are becoming even more distinct...
I don't know where the myriad souls hope to lead me...
What about you?
I hear the same.
I dismissed it as a delusion brought on by the euphoria of victory... but it seems to be more than that.
The myriad souls beckon us.
Then let's go, to wherever they beckon.
Yliš, King of Sarkaz, rests here for eternity.
Underneath a massive mountain of corpses.
In the far future, not a single soul will know there was once a King who died here.
He was so mediocre, so ordinary.
The Sarkaz do not remember the defeated.
In this war that changed their fate, the people will only remember the names of six incomparably dazzling heroes.
Here we are.
I remember this place... It was here we gathered to swear our oaths and join the battle, after Yliš's chariot was destroyed.
This is where we started...
A clothesmaker, and a guard.
Did you hear that, Theresis?
The myriad souls speak from under our feet...
Ah.
There it is.
Yes.
To think it was this small.
The crown.
With death as a witness—
As well as the myriad souls—
The twins raise the crown in unison.
The crown chooses the twins in return.
Atop the ruins, the myriad souls, whose existence remains uncertain, select two distinct hopes at a time.
...You should wear the crown, Theresa.
Why?
The King of Sarkaz is but an optimistic fantasy for the pitiful when history chooses to stay silent.
Indeed... we will change the Sarkaz's fate, with or without relying on an external force.
But in this age of despair, it will bring a glimmer of hope to our people.
I'm not fit to be that hope bringer.
My blade will cut our path forward. And you will lead the way.
...I understand.
The war has just ended. How long will we bear this crown?
Until we find a home again. Until the Black Crown swallows us into its suffering...
Until everyone under the same sky can fall into a peaceful slumber.
'Lead the way forward', you said. Do you still remember that adventure of ours?
You held your sword and carried me...
And you guided me through the bushes until we got home.
Theresa...
Allow me to crown you myself.
Alright.
Theresis...
I swear this vow to every Sarkaz...

lines.st: 'We will always be equal.'

lines.x: Erasing memories. This is something Theresa actually lacks experience in.
Not to mention she now faces a rare kind of mind.
She knows not what she will see, and has no clue if it will be dangerous. But there is no room to retreat.
She has made the final decision of her life.
She walks into the Doctor's memories, and cuts them up into countless fragments of dissipating threads.
Memory fades away in silence.
She walks through a world she has never seen and could never understand.
How did such a glorious civilization meet its end?
How do geniuses as bright as shooting stars struggle for a solution to their ultimate problem, yet find nothing?
Is resistance futile? Is unity a mere fantasy?
Is Terra's future already set in stone? What of her beloved homeland?
What of her loved ones?
She has no answer. She cannot find the answer.
She can only continue meandering in the void of memories gone.
Finally, she sees someone familiar.
Is all their enthusiasm overwhelming you?
What brings you here, Kal'tsit?
Should I not be present at this celebration of your latest victory?
It's been a long time since I last... days like these were rare back then, you know.
Or I should say, I've grown so unaccustomed to it now.
No matter, you will get used to it again, in time. Babel will be a new beginning.
Go. We should enjoy this celebration.
Theresa watches Kal'tsit pat the Doctor before gently pushing that very back into the waiting crowd.
Too many are forcing their way through the throng, hands stretched out, fighting each other to be the first to shake the Doctor's hand, or pat the Doctor's shoulder.
Some taller and gutsier ones even try to put their hands right on the Doctor's head for a good kneading.
Uh, sorry about that unruly subordinate of mine, Doctor. I wanted to stop him...
But my arm's injured... so I couldn't make it in time.
It's fine... how's your arm?
Recovery's going pretty well.
It's a pity you won't get to enjoy many drinks here tonight.
That's alright, Doctor. I'll have a few more pints on this guy's behalf.
We might have gotten ourselves stuck in that encirclement, had you not sprung that surprise attack at the end.
C'mon, let's slam a few glasses to honor the Doctor!
I'm afraid my alcohol tolerance may disappoint you, Ace.
Excuuuuse—me,—make—way please... I have to see the protagonist of the day!

So many people in here! This is the first time I feel that the command center is too small... do you want me to widen it later? There's still so many people outside hurrying in here with snacks and drinks.
Doctor, everyone's cheering for the victory you've brought us, but you seem a little... uneasy.
I thought I was masking it pretty well. *ahem* I'm just a little nervous in crowds.
Hey, careful. That's enough pushing. Amiya, you good?
I'm okay!
Doctor! Come with me, Theresa and I made you a cake!
Look, the oven was so hot I got a blister on my finger.
Thank you... Oh! Are you okay? Does anyone have some burn cream...?
A gentle cough sounds in the room, and the crowd splits into two.
Theresa sees a woman dressed in white passing by, smiling as she extends a hand to the Doctor with a still healing needle wound.
She is looking at herself.
The joy fades away, as do the memories and voices.
Theresa erases the memory she is too familiar with, and continues forward.
She sees a record she has never seen before within PRTS.
Invoke imaging capabilities. Enable administrator-level encryption.
Image number (0091_admin)...... Add subject: (This is for you).
Activating recorder... Beginning video record.
If I didn't hold on until you woke up, you'll see this video.
I will remain here, in the safest of places until everything settles.
Once I'm done with everything, I'll vanish into silence...
Disappearing before all those who hate me.
There is always a voice inside me, reminding me, asking me—'Who am I?'
Our own watcher? Or a traitor to future civilizations?
I can no longer recognize myself—
You'll get an answer after checking all the logs I left behind. But for now at least, I'll no longer hesitate to do what I should do.
When you wake up, I will have already disappeared in your past, and also unable to appear in our promised future.
That's why I hope...
...Pause recording. Delete all previous content. I... still need a bit more time.
She does not pause or sigh, just continues what she ought to do in silence.
Theresa goes deeper into the buried memories.
I need you to contact the closest rescue team! The wrecked transport has a Rim Billiton logo on it, this is your squad...
I hear someone still alive in there!
*bzzt* We got your call, but it'll take some time for us to rush over from the nearest mining plate.
Based on the information you gave us, there's been banditry at your location there before, so we'll also notify armed security to send a dispatch at once.
...Armed security...? But with the way Rim Billiton treats Infected orphans...
Hmph—
Just hold on... I'll get you out of there myself...
Please, stay with me... gah... that's a good girl, give me your hand.
Hold tight!
But... I'm so... tired...
What's your name—
Name...?
Mum... calls me... Amiya...
Amiya, just hold on tight!
I... I'm holding—
I won't let go—
The memory shatters.
This is Theresa's destination, the terminus of the Doctor's memory—
'Theresa...'
This is impossible. Every memory should have been...
'Theresa.'
She follows the voice to a void, and it speaks once more from behind the nothingness.
Where am I—
You're finally here. I've been waiting for you.
Doctor...?
Doctor?
Not many people call me that. They prefer my team codename—'Oracle'.
...Oracle...
So you're the one Kal'tsit believes in.
I've let her down terribly. But we had no choice, I'm entangled in an inescapable plot.
I've been watching you from here as you went around nurturing your hopes, as you suffered betrayal—
As if I was looking at my past self.
...I'm sorry.
You are only residue of the Doctor's burned away emotions. There's no way for me to preserve your emotions and memories in our current circumstances...
You've reached your destination, so just do what you have to do.
Thank you for trusting me.
It may be a little late for this... but I'm glad to meet you.
I'm glad to meet you too, Theresa.
Oracle walks into the distance of the endless wheat fields, disappearing within the stalks.
A draft of wind tears through the plain with a roar, and the final scene disperses until only the end of a thread is left.
Theresa holds the strand. It quickly loses its luster, and turns into powdery dust.
It's time to say goodbye...
We'll meet again in the future, won't we...?
Kal'tsit... Amiya...
......Doctor.
Theresa lets go of the final strand of thread in her hand, and watches it disperse and fade away like starlight.
She takes a step forward, towards hope...
Towards death.
